text: Code refactoring is the process of improving your code's structure without changing its behaviour. Imagine you have a messy room; refactoring is like tidying it up while keeping all your belongings in the same place. The main goal is to make the code cleaner and easier to understand. This improves readability, which helps developers work with it more efficiently. By refactoring regularly, you can reduce technical debt—the hidden cost of messy code that slows down future changes. In short, refactoring is not about fixing bugs or adding features; it’s about making your codebase healthier and more maintainable over time.
